6fee9d83-f2bc-4bfb-a9d6-274577541c6c.title: Terminal saved commands with CRUD
6fee9d83-f2bc-4bfb-a9d6-274577541c6c.details:
## Terminal Saved Commands — Implementation Plan ### Backend (Go) **`internal/services/system.go`** - Add type: ```go type SavedCommand struct { ID string `json:"id"` Name string `json:"name"` Command string `json:"command"` Order int `json:"order"` } ``` - Add to `AppState`: `SavedCommands map[string][]SavedCommand \`json:"savedCommands,omitempty"\`` - Add methods: - `GetSavedCommands(workspaceID string) []SavedCommand` — validate workspace, return deep clone - `UpsertSavedCommand(workspaceID, id, name, command string, order int) error` — create if id empty (UUID), update if exists; trim+validate inputs; clone slice before mutation; save - `DeleteSavedCommand(workspaceID, id string) error` — filter out matching id from workspace's slice; save **`internal/services/state_persistence.go`** - Update `cloneStateLocked` to deep-copy `SavedCommands` map **`internal/webserver/server.go`** - Add `"GetSavedCommands"`, `"UpsertSavedCommand"`, `"DeleteSavedCommand"` to RPC whitelist ### Regenerate bindings - Run `wails generate` ### Frontend Types **`frontend/src/app/types.ts`** ```ts export type SavedCommand = { id: string; name: string; command: string; order: number; }; ``` ### Frontend State **`frontend/src/app/state.ts`** - Add to state object: - `savedCommands: new Map()` - `savedCommandEditingId: ""` - `savedCommandDraftName: ""` - `savedCommandDraftCommand: ""` ### Frontend Backend Services **`frontend/src/backend/services.ts`** - Add: ```ts export async function GetSavedCommands(workspaceID: string): Promise { ... } export async function UpsertSavedCommand(workspaceID, id, name, command: string, order: number): Promise { ... } export async function DeleteSavedCommand(workspaceID, id: string): Promise { ... } ``` ### Frontend Render **`frontend/src/app/render.ts`** In `renderTerminalPanel`, insert between `.terminal-runs` and `.terminal-input-row`: ```html
${icons.bookmark} Saved Commands
``` Each saved command row: - Command text (clickable to run) - Edit button (✎) → switches to inline form - Delete button (✕) Inline edit form: name input + command textarea + Save/Cancel buttons. ### Frontend Actions **`frontend/src/app/actions.ts`** - `run-saved-command`: Get from saved list, set terminalDrafts, trigger run-shell-command - `add-saved-command`: Read current terminal input; if non-empty generate UUID and call UpsertSavedCommand with name = truncated command or "Untitled" - `edit-saved-command`: Set editing state for that id, populate draft fields, re-render - `delete-saved-command`: Call backend DeleteSavedCommand, re-render - `save-edited-command`: Validate inputs, call UpsertSavedCommand with existing id, clear edit state, re-render ### Frontend Patch Support **`frontend/src/app/bootstrap.ts`** - In `patchTerminalPanel`, also patch `[data-terminal-saved-list]` innerHTML so saved-command changes don't require full re-render. ### CSS **`frontend/src/styles.css`** ```css .terminal-saved-commands { /* collapsible section */ } .terminal-saved-header summary { /* styled like terminal header */ } .terminal-saved-list { /* scrollable list */ } .terminal-saved-item { /* flex row: command text + action buttons */ } .terminal-saved-run { /* main clickable area */ } .terminal-saved-edit, .terminal-saved-delete { /* small icon buttons */ } .terminal-add-saved { /* compact add button */ } /* Inline edit form styles */ /* Mobile tap target: min 36px height on interactive elements */ ``` ### Bootstrap Load **`frontend/src/app/bootstrap.ts`** - After `LoadState`, call `GetSavedCommands` for each workspace and populate `state.savedCommands`. ### Verification - Desktop: wails dev, verify saved commands appear in terminal panel - Mobile/web: verify tap targets are usable - Persistence: restart app, verify saved commands persist - `go test ./...` - `cd frontend; npm run build`

d8ae9680-11a3-4c5f-bdf2-b7827c10da35.title: Workspace setting to search parent directories for git repository
d8ae9680-11a3-4c5f-bdf2-b7827c10da35.details: Sometimes workspaces are created in a sub-folder of a git repository. Currently, this means that the git feature will not work. We should add a per-workspace setting to recursively check for a git repository on a parent directory until one is found. If one is found, it should be stored into the `.echo/workspace.json` file so it doesn't need to do the recursive check every time git is invoked.

f1b4a109-2674-4971-a964-2b688f090754.title: Fix saved command dialog obscured by bottom nav on mobile
f1b4a109-2674-4971-a964-2b688f090754.details:
## Problem
On mobile (≤720px), the Save/Cancel buttons in the saved command dialog (.saved-command-dialog) can be hidden behind the fixed bottom navigation bar (.mobile-bottom-nav).

## Root Cause
The dialog overlay uses `place-items: center` for vertical centering, which doesn't account for the ~64px fixed bottom nav. No max-height constraint exists on mobile (unlike Kanban modals which were previously fixed).

## Change
**File:** `echo/frontend/src/styles.css` — inside existing `@media (max-width: 720px)` block (~line 9606):

Add to `.saved-command-dialog`:
- `max-height: calc(100vh - 96px);` — leaves breathing room above the bottom nav
- `overflow-y: auto;` — scrolls content if dialog exceeds max-height

This mirrors the existing pattern for Kanban modals (.card-detail, .kanban-card-create-dialog) which use the same constraint in this breakpoint.

## Verification
- `cd frontend; npm run build` — TypeScript/Vite build passes
- Manual: on mobile viewport (≤720px), open terminal → click "Save current" → Save/Cancel buttons should be visible above the bottom nav bar

fb940a9c-0e45-4f02-ad37-0cb695fc0bb7.title: Enforce mandatory Kanban fields on backlog task creation
fb940a9c-0e45-4f02-ad37-0cb695fc0bb7.details:
## Problem

When an agent creates a backlog task (via `workspace_task_create`), it often omits the fields that `CreateReadyKanbanCard` requires. Later, when the user tries to create a Kanban card from that task — either via the "Create Kanban Card" button in the UI or through `CreateReadyKanbanCard(workspaceID, title, description, acceptanceCriteria)` — the call is rejected with errors like:

- `"description is required"`
- `"at least one acceptance criterion is required"`

This forces the user to fill in the missing fields manually instead of the agent doing it upfront.

## Requirement

Every future backlog task created via `workspace_task_create` MUST include all three Kanban-card-required fields:

1. **`title`** — concise imperative phrase (already a required parameter)
2. **`details`** — concrete implementation description, not just one-liners; must be non-empty after trim
3. **`acceptanceCriteria`** — at least one observable, verifiable acceptance criterion array entry

If any of these are missing or effectively empty on task creation, the agent should treat it as incomplete and refine the task rather than leaving a half-baked backlog item.

## Validation reference

The backend validation in `internal/services/kanban.go:CreateReadyKanbanCard` rejects cards when:
- `title == ""` (after trim)
- `description == ""` (after trim)
- `acceptanceCriteria` has zero non-empty entries after trimming each element

The agent prompt or system instructions should mirror these same rules at the point of `workspace_task_create` so that every task in `.echo/backlog.md` is immediately convertible to a Kanban card without user intervention.

## Scope

This applies to:
- Agent-created tasks (primary target — the decomposer/agent calling `workspace_task_create`)
- It may also inform prompt updates in `internal/services/decomposition.go` or workspace skill notes to reinforce the rule at creation time, not just at conversion time.
acceptanceCriteria: New backlog tasks created via workspace_task_create include non-empty title, details (description), and at least one acceptance criterion
The agent refuses to create a task if any of the three required Kanban fields are missing or effectively empty after trimming
An existing backlog task with missing fields can be retroactively updated to include all three before creating a Kanban card from it
The rejection errors from CreateReadyKanbanCard (title is required, description is required, at least one acceptance criterion is required) are prevented by enforcement at task-creation time
